subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Particle on x-axis — Acceleration, Position, Displacement vs Distance & Matching Velocities
stimulus: A particle moves along the $x$-axis with velocity given by $v(t) = \frac{10\sin(0.4t^2)}{2t^2 - t + 3}$ for time $0 \le t \le 3.5$. The particle is at position $x = 5$ at time $t = 0$.

parts.prompt: Find the acceleration of the particle at time $t = 3$.
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $v'(3) \approx -2.118$. The acceleration of the particle at $t = 3$ is approximately $-2.118$.

parts.prompt: Find the position of the particle at time $t = 3$.
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $x(3) = 5 + \int_0^3 v(t)\, dt \approx 5 + (-6.760) = -1.760$. (The integral $\int_0^3 v(t)\, dt \approx -6.760$.)

parts.prompt: Evaluate $\int_0^{3.5} v(t)\, dt$ and $\int_0^{3.5} |v(t)|\, dt$. Interpret the meaning of each integral in the context of the problem.
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $\int_0^{3.5} v(t)\, dt \approx 2.844$ is the displacement; $\int_0^{3.5} |v(t)|\, dt \approx 3.737$ is the total distance traveled, over $0 \le t \le 3.5$.

scoring_points.description: Correct interpretation of $\int_0^{3.5} v(t)\, dt$: the displacement (change in position) of the particle over $0 \le t \le 3.5$

required_elements: Correct interpretation of $\int_0^{3.5} v(t)\, dt$: the displacement (change in position) of the particle over $0 \le t \le 3.5$

scoring_points.description: Correct interpretation of $\int_0^{3.5} |v(t)|\, dt$: the total distance traveled by the particle over $0 \le t \le 3.5$

required_elements: Correct interpretation of $\int_0^{3.5} |v(t)|\, dt$: the total distance traveled by the particle over $0 \le t \le 3.5$

parts.prompt: A second particle moves along the $x$-axis with position given by $x_2(t) = t^2 - t$ for $0 \le t \le 3.5$. At what time $t$ are the two particles moving with the same velocity?
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $v(t) = 2t - 1 \Rightarrow t \approx 1.571$ (or $1.570$). The two particles move with the same velocity at $t \approx 1.571$.
